description_long: This is a rustic, wooden bar building with a distinctive Old West saloon appearance. The facade features a wide, flat awning supported by posts, and above it, a sign reading 'Saloon' is integrated into the woodwork. Two rectangular windows flank the central entrance, which is a large, open doorway without a visible door. The structure is primarily constructed from weathered wooden planks, giving it a rough, aged texture. The rear of the building is plain, with no windows or doors, and a wooden platform extends from the back. The overall design is simple yet iconic, evoking the atmosphere of a frontier town.
description: A rustic wooden saloon-style bar with a wide awning, central open entrance, and two front windows, featuring weathered wood and Old West architectural details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the awning, a single window, and the rear platform extending outward.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the 'Saloon' sign, two symmetrical windows, the wide awning, and the central open entrance.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the window and the rear platform from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain wooden back wall and the full extent of the rear platform, with no windows or doors visible.
attribute_signage: Raised wooden 'Saloon' text sign, centered above the entrance, integrated into the facade, unlit and unadorned.
attribute_entrance_type: Large open central doorway with no visible door, covered by a wide wooden awning.
attribute_window_arrangement: Two rectangular windows symmetrically placed on either side of the entrance, framed in wood, with simple clear panes.
